2 Collaboration rules (simple)

Pull before you start work.
Commit small changes with clear messages.
Only one person edits the same .qmd file at a time.
Use Issues (optional) for tasks/bugs.

title: Differences in Gut Microbiota Composition and Relative Abundance of TMA-Producing Bacterial Taxa in Vegetarians v. Omnivores
text: Write 4–6 sentences describing: - Research Question: Do vegetarians and omnivores differ in gut microbiota composition and in the relative abundance of bacterial taxa associated with microbial TMA production? - Hypothesis: Omnivores will have a higher relative abundance of gut microbial taxa associated with microbial TMA production compared to vegetarians. - A collection of 16s rDNA amplicon of human vegetarian - Dataset is obtained from NCBI, and contains multiple 300-301 long DNA sequences for 57 healthy volunteers based on fecal sample. The raw sequences are a collection of 16s rDNA amplicon of human vegetarian/ominovores. - your approach (methods) - We intend to deliver a collection of figures including a stacked bar plot (to llustrate the bacterial composition of the different diet groups), a boxplot (to compare the relative abundance of TMA-associated bacteria by diet group), an Alpha diversity plot (showcasing diversity analysis (Shannon index) between diet groups), and a PCoA Plot (showcasing visualization of differences between the diet groups based off of their gut bacteria), as well as a collection of tables including one to summarize the samples, one to illustrate levels of TMA-related bacteria, and one to showcasing statistical test results.
